398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits

230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes

261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:

22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)

109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)

121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166

168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234

236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)

Perfect! I have gathered comprehensive information about how top_p and top_k sampling parameters are implemented in vLLM. Let me provide you with a detailed analysis:

## Summary

I've found and analyzed the key files implementing top_p and top_k sampling parameters in vLLM:

### 1. **SamplingParams Class** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/vllm/sampling_params.py`)

#### Field Definitions (Lines 212-213):
```python
top_p: float = 1.0
top_k: int = 0
```

#### Documentation in Docstring (Lines 150-153):
```
top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
    to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
    to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
```

#### Validation (`_verify_args` method, Lines 387-428):

**For top_p (Lines 417-418):**
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
```

**For top_k (Lines 419-425):**
```python
# quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
if self.top_k < -1:
    raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                     f"got {self.top_k}.")
if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
    raise TypeError(
        f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
```

#### Reset on Greedy Sampling (Lines 377-382):
When temperature is near zero (greedy sampling), top_p and top_k are automatically reset:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

#### Default Factory Method (`from_optional`, Lines 251-325):
```python
top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
top_k=top_k,
```

---

### 2. **Sampling Metadata Data Structures** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`)

#### SequenceGroupToSample Class (Lines 19-62):
This dataclass carries sampling parameters through the sampling pipeline:
```python
@dataclass
class SequenceGroupToSample:
    seq_ids: list[int]
    sampling_params: SamplingParams  # <-- Contains top_p, top_k
    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
    seq_len: Optional[int]
    query_len: Optional[int]
    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
    is_prompt: bool
    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
    sample_indices: list[int]
```

#### SamplingTensors Class (Lines 372-597):
This structure converts sampling parameters to GPU tensors:
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor      # <-- top_p as tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor      # <-- top_k as tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

#### Parameter Extraction in `from_sampling_metadata` (Lines 387-495):

The method extracts top_p and top_k from SamplingParams (Lines 415-420):
```python
top_p = sampling_params.top_p
min_p = sampling_params.min_p

# k should not be greater than the vocab size.
top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
```

Logic to determine if sampling is needed (Lines 426-428):
```python
if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                           or top_k != vocab_size):
    do_top_p_top_k = True
```

---

### 3. **Key Implementation Details**

**From sampling_params.py:**
- Constants defined at line 20: `_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5`
- top_p must be strictly in the range (0, 1] (exclusive of 0, inclusive of 1)
- top_k can be 0, -1 (disabled), or any integer ≥ 1
- When temperature is very low (< 1e-5), both top_p and top_k are reset to disable them

**From sampling_metadata.py:**
- Line 16 defines `_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5` (same threshold as sampling_params)
- top_k is clamped to vocab_size (line 419)
- top_k is treated as "all tokens" if less than 1 (line 420)
- Sampling tensors are created on CPU with pinned memory for efficient GPU transfer (lines 545-550, 575-580)

---

### 4. **Parameter Flow Through System**

1. **Input**: `SamplingParams` with `top_p` and `top_k` fields
2. **Metadata Layer**: `SequenceGroupToSample` carries the sampling params through the pipeline
3. **Tensor Conversion**: `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` extracts top_p/top_k and converts them to GPU tensors
4. **Sampling**: The tensors are used in the actual sampling kernels

All relevant file paths and code snippets with line numbers are provided above.

21	from vllm.sequence import (VLLM_INVALID_TOKEN_ID,
22	                           CompletionSequenceGroupOutput, Logprob,
23	                           PromptLogprobs, SampleLogprobs, SequenceOutput)

25	
26	if envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER and find_spec("flashinfer"):
27	    # yapf: disable
28	    from flashinfer.sampling import (
29	        top_k_top_p_sampling_from_probs as flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling)
30	
31	    # yapf: enable

33	    flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling = None

35	from vllm.logger import init_logger

37	logger = init_logger(__name__)
38

40	def get_sampler() -> torch.nn.Module:
41	    if envs.VLLM_USE_V1:
42	        # Lazy import: the v1 package isn't distributed
43	        from vllm.v1.sample.sampler import Sampler as V1Sampler
44	        return V1Sampler()
45	    return Sampler()
46	
47	
48	# (num_token_ids, num_parent_ids) per sequence group.
49	SampleResultType = list[tuple[list[int], list[int]]]
50	
51	# Types of temporary data structures used for
52	# computing sample_result
53	SampleMetadataType = dict[SamplingType, tuple[list[int],
54	                                              list[SequenceGroupToSample]]]
55	MultinomialSamplesType = dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor]
56	SampleResultsDictType = dict[int, tuple[list[int], list[int]]]
57	
58	
59	# Encapsulates temporary data structures for computing
60	# sample_result.
61	#
62	# * For multi-step scheduling: must be returned
63	#   by `Sampler.forward()` and used later to compute the pythonized
64	#   sample_result
65	#
66	# * For single-step scheduling: consumed immediately
67	#   inside `Sampler.forward()` to compute pythonized sample_result.
68	@dataclass
69	class SampleResultArgsType:
70	    sample_metadata: SampleMetadataType
71	    multinomial_samples: MultinomialSamplesType
72	    sample_results_dict: SampleResultsDictType
73	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata
74	    greedy_samples: Optional[torch.Tensor]
75	
76	
77	# Union of non-deferred (single-step scheduling)
78	# vs deferred (multi-step scheduling)
79	# sample result types
80	MaybeDeferredSampleResultType = Union[SampleResultType, SampleResultArgsType]
81	
82	# Abbreviation of the _sample() return type
83	SampleReturnType = tuple[MaybeDeferredSampleResultType, Optional[torch.Tensor]]
84	
85	
86	class SamplerOutput(
87	        msgspec.Struct,
88	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
89	        array_like=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
90	    """For each sequence group, we generate a list of SequenceOutput object,
91	    each of which contains one possible candidate for the next token.
92	
93	    This data structure implements methods, so it can be used like a list, but
94	    also has optional fields for device tensors.
95	    """
96	
97	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]
98	
99	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.
100	    sampled_token_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
101	
102	    # On-device tensor containing the logprobs of each token.
103	    logprobs: Optional["torch.Tensor"] = None
104	
105	    # Holds either (1) the pythonized sampler result (single-step scheduling)
106	    # or (2) what will be arguments for later deferred pythonization of the
107	    # sampler result (muliti-step scheduling)
108	    deferred_sample_results_args: Optional[SampleResultArgsType] = None
109	
110	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.
111	    sampled_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
112	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
113	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be
114	    # 'broadcasted' to all other PP ranks for next step.
115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
116	
117	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token embeddings (embeddings
118	    # corresponding to the sampled token ids). Used when prompt embeddings are
119	    # specified in lieu of prompt token ids or text.
120	    sampled_token_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
121	
122	    # Spec decode metrics populated by workers.
123	    spec_decode_worker_metrics: Optional[SpecDecodeWorkerMetrics] = None
124	
125	    # Optional last hidden states from the model.
126	    hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
127	
128	    # Optional prefill hidden states from the model
129	    # (used for models like EAGLE).
130	    prefill_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
131	
132	    # Time taken in the forward pass for this across all workers
133	    model_forward_time: Optional[float] = None
134	
135	    # Time taken in the model execute function. This will include model forward,
136	    # block/sync across workers, cpu-gpu sync time and sampling time.
137	    model_execute_time: Optional[float] = None
138	
139	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> CompletionSequenceGroupOutput:
140	        return self.outputs[idx]
141	
142	    def __setitem__(self, idx: int, value):
143	        self.outputs[idx] = value
144	
145	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]:
146	        return iter(self.outputs)
147	
148	    def __len__(self):
149	        return len(self.outputs)
150	
151	    def __eq__(self, other: object):
152	        return isinstance(other,
153	                          self.__class__) and self.outputs == other.outputs
154	
155	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
156	        """Show the shape of a tensor instead of its values to reduce noise.
157	        """
158	        sampled_token_probs_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_probs is None
159	                                    else self.sampled_token_probs.shape)
160	        sampled_token_ids_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_ids is None else
161	                                  self.sampled_token_ids.shape)
162	        return (
163	            f"SamplerOutput(outputs={self.outputs}, "
164	            f"sampled_token_probs={sampled_token_probs_repr}, "
165	            f"sampled_token_ids={sampled_token_ids_repr}, "
166	            f"spec_decode_worker_metrics={self.spec_decode_worker_metrics})")
167	
168	
169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.

135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they

140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1

146	            the model to repeat tokens.

387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len

## Summary: Top_p and Top_k Sampling Filtering Implementation in vLLM

I've explored the vLLM sampler layer and found the complete top_p and top_k filtering implementation. Here are the key findings:

### Primary Files for Filtering Logic

#### 1. **Main v0 Sampler Implementation**
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**Key Function**: `_apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 398-424)
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Forward Pass Flow** (lines 225-295):
1. Line 282: Apply temperature scaling
2. Line 284-286: Call `_apply_top_k_top_p()` if needed (unless using FlashInfer)
3. Line 289: Apply min_p filtering if needed
4. Line 293-295: Compute probabilities and log probabilities from filtered logits

#### 2. **V1 Sampler (New Architecture)**
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

This file contains multiple implementations:

**A. `apply_top_k_top_p()` function (lines 168-208)** - PyTorch-native version:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits."""
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k.
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p.
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**B. `apply_top_k_only()` function (lines 211-233)** - Optimized for top_k only:
Uses `torch.topk()` instead of full sort to avoid sorting entire vocab

**C. `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` function (lines 121-165)** - TPU-optimized version:
Uses cumulative probability cutoff approach instead of scatter (slow on TPU)

#### 3. **Sampling Metadata and Tensors**
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**Class**: `SamplingTensors` (lines 373-385) - Holds the per-sample parameters:
- `top_ps`: torch.Tensor (nucleus sampling probability threshold)
- `top_ks`: torch.Tensor (top-k sampling count threshold)
- `temperatures`: torch.Tensor
- `min_ps`: torch.Tensor
- `presence_penalties`, `frequency_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`: torch.Tensor
- `prompt_tokens`, `output_tokens`: torch.Tensor

**Method**: `from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 387-495) - Initializes tensors and determines which filters need to be applied

### Filtering Logic Breakdown

**Top_k Filtering** (Applied in both v0 and v1):
1. Sort logits in ascending order
2. Get the k-th largest value (index = vocab_size - k)
3. Create mask where logits < top_k_threshold
4. Set masked positions to -inf (zero probability)

**Top_p Filtering** (Applied in both v0 and v1):
1. Convert sorted logits to probabilities via softmax
2. Compute cumulative sum of probabilities
3. Create mask where cumsum <= (1 - p) (i.e., keep top probabilities that sum to at least p)
4. Ensure at least one token remains: `mask[:, -1] = False`
5. Set masked positions to -inf
6. Scatter back to original logit order

**Additional Filtering** (Lines 288-289 in v0 sampler):
- `_apply_min_p()`: Removes tokens with probability < min_p * max_probability

### Entry Points

1. **v0 Sampler**: `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - `Sampler.forward()`
2. **v1 Sampler**: `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - `Sampler.forward()` which calls `TopKTopPSampler`
3. **FlashInfer Fallback** (Optional, high-performance CUDA kernel): Uses `flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling()` or `flashinfer_sample()`

### Supporting Files

- **CUDA Kernel Implementation**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/csrc/sampler.cu` (contains `apply_repetition_penalties_kernel` and related penalty kernels)
- **Penalty Application**: `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` (contains `apply_penalties()` function)

The flow is: **Raw Logits → Temperature Scaling → Top_k/Top_p Filtering → Min_p Filtering → Softmax → Probabilities → Multinomial Sampling**

## Summary: Top_p and Top_k Sampling Filtering Implementation in vLLM

I've explored the vLLM sampler layer and found the complete top_p and top_k filtering implementation. Here are the key findings:

### Primary Files for Filtering Logic

#### 1. **Main v0 Sampler Implementation**
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**Key Function**: `_apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 398-424)
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Forward Pass Flow** (lines 225-295):
1. Line 282: Apply temperature scaling
2. Line 284-286: Call `_apply_top_k_top_p()` if needed (unless using FlashInfer)
3. Line 289: Apply min_p filtering if needed
4. Line 293-295: Compute probabilities and log probabilities from filtered logits

#### 2. **V1 Sampler (New Architecture)**
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

This file contains multiple implementations:

**A. `apply_top_k_top_p()` function (lines 168-208)** - PyTorch-native version:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits."""
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k.
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p.
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**B. `apply_top_k_only()` function (lines 211-233)** - Optimized for top_k only:
Uses `torch.topk()` instead of full sort to avoid sorting entire vocab

**C. `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` function (lines 121-165)** - TPU-optimized version:
Uses cumulative probability cutoff approach instead of scatter (slow on TPU)

#### 3. **Sampling Metadata and Tensors**
**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**Class**: `SamplingTensors` (lines 373-385) - Holds the per-sample parameters:
- `top_ps`: torch.Tensor (nucleus sampling probability threshold)
- `top_ks`: torch.Tensor (top-k sampling count threshold)
- `temperatures`: torch.Tensor
- `min_ps`: torch.Tensor
- `presence_penalties`, `frequency_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`: torch.Tensor
- `prompt_tokens`, `output_tokens`: torch.Tensor

**Method**: `from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 387-495) - Initializes tensors and determines which filters need to be applied

### Filtering Logic Breakdown

**Top_k Filtering** (Applied in both v0 and v1):
1. Sort logits in ascending order
2. Get the k-th largest value (index = vocab_size - k)
3. Create mask where logits < top_k_threshold
4. Set masked positions to -inf (zero probability)

**Top_p Filtering** (Applied in both v0 and v1):
1. Convert sorted logits to probabilities via softmax
2. Compute cumulative sum of probabilities
3. Create mask where cumsum <= (1 - p) (i.e., keep top probabilities that sum to at least p)
4. Ensure at least one token remains: `mask[:, -1] = False`
5. Set masked positions to -inf
6. Scatter back to original logit order

**Additional Filtering** (Lines 288-289 in v0 sampler):
- `_apply_min_p()`: Removes tokens with probability < min_p * max_probability

### Entry Points

1. **v0 Sampler**: `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - `Sampler.forward()`
2. **v1 Sampler**: `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - `Sampler.forward()` which calls `TopKTopPSampler`
3. **FlashInfer Fallback** (Optional, high-performance CUDA kernel): Uses `flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling()` or `flashinfer_sample()`

### Supporting Files

- **CUDA Kernel Implementation**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331350395-xy12mm/csrc/sampler.cu` (contains `apply_repetition_penalties_kernel` and related penalty kernels)
- **Penalty Application**: `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` (contains `apply_penalties()` function)

The flow is: **Raw Logits → Temperature Scaling → Top_k/Top_p Filtering → Min_p Filtering → Softmax → Probabilities → Multinomial Sampling**

def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,      # top_ps from SamplingTensors

284:        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285:            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286:                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

**Actual Sampling Function (lines 398-424):**
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,      # top_ps from SamplingTensors
    k: torch.Tensor,      # top_ks from SamplingTensors
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Apply top-k (lines 405-410)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Apply top-p (lines 412-418)
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
```

372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

498	    def from_lists(
499	        cls,

501	        top_ps: list[float],

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
        frequency_penalties: list[float],
        repetition_penalties: list[float],
        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.
        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens

        if do_penalties:
            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                prompt_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                output_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
        else:
            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            prompt_t = empty_tensor
            output_t = empty_tensor

        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
            temperatures,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            min_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            presence_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            frequency_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            repetition_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ks,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
        # transfer to device.

        return cls(
            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                       non_blocking=True),
            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                         non_blocking=True),
            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                           non_blocking=True),
            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        )

# Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
        # Compute the log probabilities.
        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

        # Sample the next tokens.
        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
            probs,
            logprobs,
            sampling_metadata,
            sampling_tensors,
            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
        )

        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
        else:
            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
            # GPU-side tensors.
            on_device_tensors = None

        # Get the logprobs query results.
        prompt_logprobs = None
        sample_logprobs = None
        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
                                  SampleResultArgsType)
            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)

        return _build_sampler_output(
            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
            sampling_metadata,
            prompt_logprobs,
            sample_logprobs,
            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

    @property
    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
        the greedily-sampled token.

        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
        sampled token to 1.

        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
        """
        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace


def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
        have not been generated yet
    """
    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
    logits_applied = 0
    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params

        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            continue

        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)

            if seqs_to_penalize:
                # convert to the index into logits
                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
                logits_to_penalize.extend(
                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))

    if logits_to_penalize:
        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")

    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
    return logits


def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.sample.logits_processor import LogitsProcessorManager


@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:

    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool

    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]

    # None means no logprobs, 0 means sampled token logprobs only
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]

    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor

    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]

    # `allowed_token_ids_mask` is a 2D bool tensor of shape (max batch size,
    # vocab size).
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]

    # Loaded logits processors
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager

def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

    Args:
        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
            seq_group.do_sample is False.
    Returns:
        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
    """
    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
    sample_idx = 0
    results: SampleResultType = []
    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            results.append(([], []))
            continue

        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
    return results

if p is not None:
        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one

        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))

def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.

    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
    which can be slow for large batches.

    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
    """
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits

        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k.
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
        # Get all the top_k values.
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p.
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        # at least one
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def apply_top_k_only(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Apply top-k mask to the logits.

    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.

    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
    """
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    # Handle non-topk rows.
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits


def random_sample(
    probs: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.

    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
    """
    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
    # that have their own seeds.
    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
        q.exponential_()
    if generators:
        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
        # one by one. Optimize this.

# Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation

        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:

    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
        if self.n < 1:
            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
        if self.best_of is not None:
            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
            if self.best_of < 1:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
        if self.top_k < -1:
            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
            raise TypeError(
                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                             f"{self.min_p}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
        if self.min_tokens < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
